authenticode.signer_subject: Sony Pictures Entertainment Inc., Culver City, California, US
authenticode.issuer: DigiCert Assured ID Code Signing CA-1
authenticode.note: the certificate was stolen during the Sony breach and either re-used by attackers or by researchers; the cert appears in dumped credentials posted publicly in late November 2014

imports_obfuscated_runtime.encoding: ASCII API name interleaved with single-character spaces and full-stops; the loader strips both classes of junk before passing the cleaned name to GetProcAddress

c2_servers.note: open relay used in the SPE intrusion; same IP appeared in DarkSeoul wiper traffic earlier in 2014

c2_loop.note_on_table_size: Init loop seeds slots 0..9 with 0.0.0.0 wide-string. Slots 0 and 1 are then overwritten with the live IPs. The selector uses rand() mod 10, so all 10 slots are reachable; placeholder slots 2..9 are operator-fillable in custom builds.

key_functions.name: main
key_functions.purpose: config init, seed PRNG, kick off C2 loop

key_functions.name: copy_wide_string
key_functions.purpose: ASCII to wide-char copy used to seed the 40-entry server table

key_functions.name: trigger_date_check
key_functions.purpose: compare GetSystemTime fields against constants at 0x4140xx; calls payload-trigger fcn.0040858a on match

key_functions.name: c2_main_loop
key_functions.purpose: rotate through server table, retry/sleep, dispatch commands

key_functions.name: c2_attempt_connect
key_functions.purpose: single-shot connect-and-handshake against a server table entry

key_functions.name: tcp_send
key_functions.purpose: send wrapper used by command-output channel

key_functions.name: drive_enumerator
key_functions.purpose: iterate drive letters C: through Z: via GetDriveTypeW + GetDiskFreeSpaceExW

key_functions.name: command_dispatcher
key_functions.purpose: branch on opcode received over TCP

obfuscation.string_table_dot_space: API names stored in .rdata as ASCII strings interleaved with junk dots and spaces; e.g. 'Vir . tualFr. ee' decodes to VirtualFree. The runtime decoder strips both 0x20 and 0x2E before calling GetProcAddress.
obfuscation.version_info_masquerade: VS_VERSION_INFO claims to be Intel graphics persistence helper igfxtpers.exe v5.6.4590.2023, copyrighted to Microsoft Corporation
obfuscation.no_packer: the binary is not packed - sections show normal compiled-C entropy; obfuscation is at the string and import-resolution layer only

persistence: service installation via OpenSCManagerW + CreateServiceW (dynamically resolved)
registers SCM control handler so the binary can be configured to run as SYSTEM service
discovery: drive enumeration C:..Z: with type and free-space
OS-version detection (rich product table from Windows 2000 through Server 2012 R2)
execution: shell exec via cmd.exe redirected to a temporary file (format string '%sd.e%sc "%s > %s" 2>&1')
command_and_control: TCP outbound on port 443 to two hard-coded IPs
rotating retry loop with 60-second sleeps, max 5 attempts per server, 10 rotations total
Authenticode-signed binary masquerades as a Windows Update component to evade gateway inspection
destructive: drive enumerator wired into the command dispatcher
DeleteFileW + SetFilePointer + WriteFile primitives present and reachable from C2 commands
this binary is the foothold/RAT component; the destructive raw-disk overwrite was historically delivered by a companion driver (EldoS RawDisk or 'usbdrv3.sys' style) dropped after staging
anti_analysis: stolen Authenticode signature for code-signing trust
system-time gated trigger of payload routine

techniques.name: Create or Modify System Process: Windows Service
Command and Scripting Interpreter: Windows Command Shell
Obfuscated Files or Information: Encrypted/Encoded File
Masquerading: Match Legitimate Name or Location

certificate.subject: Sony Pictures Entertainment Inc.
certificate.issuer: DigiCert Assured ID Code Signing CA-1

weaknesses: Hard-coded C2 IPs - no DGA, no fast-flux; once both addresses sinkhole the malware degrades into useless retry loops
Uniform 60-second sleep with no jitter generates a regular network beacon visible at gateway level
Dot-space API obfuscation is trivial to defeat by stripping the two filler characters; static signatures match the cleartext after one regex pass
VS_VERSION_INFO masquerade collides with real Intel graphics binaries in product-version space; mismatch between claimed version 5.6.4590.2023 and actual file size easy to flag
Stolen-certificate signature is the strongest evasion lever and the most fragile - a single revocation event invalidates every signed sample
No AV/AVI checks, no debugger checks, no VM checks: the binary will detonate happily inside any sandbox that allows TCP egress on 443
OS detection table does not branch on Windows 10 or later - the build was frozen mid-2014
